evidence_quotes.quote: Publishers permanently disabling video games they have already sold.
deliberately prevent customers from retaining or repairing their purchase.
Include regulation that enforce continuation of the lives of video games that currently have no end of life plan beyond complete inaccessibility of even the most basic form of the game.

paragraphs.text: Hello. I am in support of your movement, however I would like to request you also include the continued usability of video games of all kinds to a much wider extent. This could be worded like 6. Publishers permanently disabling video games they have already sold. Some publishers are selling video games under terms that may already violate Directive 93/ 13/EEC and deliberately prevent customers from retaining or repairing their purchase. I feel it is very unfair and unfriendly treatment of consumers that the wide majority of games today, are and will be made completely inaccessible when video games makers, and publishers. On the StopKillingGames Dead game list are a large list of documented games that at risk or have been destroyed. Many games I play are on that list, and I fear that my favourite games will die. Therefore, I am asking for the act to: Include regulation that enforce continuation of the lives of video games that currently have no end of life plan beyond complete inaccessibility of even the most basic form of the game. This continued support could be done by the player community of the individual video games. I fully believe that the Stop Destroying Video-games EU initiative that currently has over 1,000,000 signatures, and Stop Killing Games movement is approaching this problem very well, but it is not far-reaching enough. Since the movement and partition only would include games created after the partition has been passed. I would like to see action taken to implement after end of life support for all games currently supported and potentially even recently unsupported games
